title: chore(deps): update dependency eslint-import-resolver-webpack to v0.10.1
body:
This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Type | Update | Change | References |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| eslint-import-resolver-webpack | devDependencies | minor | `0.8.4` -> `0.10.1` | [source](https://renovatebot.com/gh/benmosher/eslint-plugin-import) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>benmosher/eslint-plugin-import</summary>

### [`v0.10.1`](https://renovatebot.com/gh/benmosher/eslint-plugin-import/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#Earlier-releases-0101-and-younger)

[Compare Source](https://renovatebot.com/gh/benmosher/eslint-plugin-import/compare/v0.10.0...v0.10.1)

See [GitHub release notes](https://renovatebot.com/gh/benmosher/eslint-plugin-import/releases?after=v0.11.0)
for info on changes for earlier releases.

### [`v0.10.0`](https://renovatebot.com/gh/benmosher/eslint-plugin-import/releases/v0.10.0)

Breaking: removed `no-errors` rule. Instead, each individual rule will report parse errors in the target imported file, if encountered.

[#​90](https://renovatebot.com/gh/benmosher/eslint-plugin-import/issues/90): Added `{commonjs: [bool], amd: [bool]}` option object to `no-unresolved`. If set `true`, will attempt to resolve module paths for CommonJS `require` and AMD `define` + `require` in a limited set of cases. Not nearly so smart as Webpack, but smart enough to be useful. (hopefully.) Thanks [@​mctep](https://renovatebot.com/gh/mctep) for changing my mind on this. 😁

[#​94](https://renovatebot.com/gh/benmosher/eslint-plugin-import/issues/94): Dependency parser will infer 'jsx' plugin if using default Babylon and `jsx` is asserted in the `ecmaFeatures`. Thanks [@​jameslnewell](https://renovatebot.com/gh/jameslnewell) for bringing this up.

[#​88](https://renovatebot.com/gh/benmosher/eslint-plugin-import/issues/88): un-smarted `no-require`. It will now report on all `require` statements, everywhere, regardless of target.

</details>
